title: The Gang Saves the Day

jokes.text: Don't grab a beer. We're on our way to a bar, which we own.
jokes.setup: Gang wanting to buy beer at convenience store
jokes.punchline: Logical observation that they own a bar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's occasional rational thoughts

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Don't grab a beer. We're on our way to a bar, which we own.

jokes.text: All right, well, I'm not gonna be the only one not drinking beer, so come on.
jokes.setup: Charlie's logical argument against buying beer
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves to peer pressure despite his own logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how the gang corrupts each other

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: All right, well, I'm not gonna be the only one not drinking beer, so come on.

jokes.text: Hey, bozo. Walk away now, and I let you live... or stick around and repent for your sins.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to be a tough action hero
jokes.punchline: Awkward religious phrasing mixed with tough guy talk
jokes.explanation: Great character integration showing Mac's religious obsession even in action scenarios

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hey, bozo. Walk away now, and I let you live... or stick around and repent for your sins.

jokes.text: And you better say 'cheese.'
jokes.setup: Mac trying to deliver a cool one-liner
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun that makes no sense in context
jokes.explanation: The badness of the pun is the joke - Mac's incompetence at being cool

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: And you better say 'cheese.'

jokes.text: Did you like my 'say cheese' line? I didn't quite get that, if I'm being honest.
jokes.setup: Mac proud of his terrible pun
jokes.punchline: Charlie's honest confusion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Mac's delusion meets reality

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Did you like my 'say cheese' line?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I didn't quite get that, if I'm being honest.

jokes.text: It was a pun, 'cause I flung cheese in his face, you know?
jokes.setup: Mac trying to explain his brilliant wordplay
jokes.punchline: Reveals he doesn't understand what a pun is
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac moment - confident in his ignorance

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It was a pun, 'cause I flung cheese in his face, you know?

jokes.text: No, if you... if you had taken his photograph, and then you flung cheese in his face... that would be a pun.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining what would actually make it a pun
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction that's technically right but misses the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - condescending but accurate

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, if you... if you had taken his photograph, and then you flung cheese in his face... that would be a pun.

jokes.text: Should I go get a camera?
jokes.setup: Dennis explains how the pun would work with a camera
jokes.punchline: Mac wants to literally recreate the scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Mac's misunderstanding and literal thinking

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Should I go get a camera?

jokes.text: Yakuza.
jokes.setup: Gang discovers the robber's identity
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to international crime syndicate
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation beat - one word that changes everything

dialogue.line: Yakuza.

jokes.text: And that's a great pun, dude. You used the pun right! Yeah, you got the pun right, dude.
jokes.setup: Mac says 'Cool off' while kicking someone into a fridge
jokes.punchline: Gang excited he finally made an actual pun
jokes.explanation: Great callback that pays off the earlier setup perfectly

dialogue.line: And that's a great pun, dude. You used the pun right! Yeah, you got the pun right, dude.

jokes.text: What pun? The... 'Cool off' and you kicked him in a fridge. He's not getting it, is he?
jokes.setup: Mac successfully made a pun
jokes.punchline: He doesn't even realize he did it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Mac accidentally succeeds but doesn't know it

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What pun? The... 'Cool off' and you kicked him in a fridge.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He's not getting it, is he?

jokes.text: What is a pun?
jokes.setup: Extended discussion about puns
jokes.punchline: Mac admits total ignorance despite being part of the whole conversation
jokes.explanation: Good button to the running gag about Mac's confusion

dialogue.line: What is a pun?

jokes.text: I killed the boss. That's not the boss, you idiot. He's my father!
jokes.setup: Mac proud of killing the crime boss
jokes.punchline: Reveals he killed an innocent store owner who's someone's father
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation that's both shocking and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I killed the boss.

dialogue.speaker: Store Owner
dialogue.line: That's not the boss, you idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Store Owner
dialogue.line: He's my father!

jokes.text: Whoops. My bad.
jokes.setup: Mac just learned he murdered an innocent man
jokes.punchline: Casual, dismissive response to killing someone
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan understatement for a horrific situation - peak dark comedy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Whoops. My bad.

jokes.text: Now, is your throat okay? Is it... is that from the smoke, or...? Is that a pun?
jokes.setup: Store owner is choking/dying from smoke
jokes.punchline: Mac thinks the dying man's words might be a pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Mac's complete tone-deafness and obsession

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Now, is your throat okay? Is it... is that from the smoke, or...? Is that a pun?

jokes.text: I... don't... forgive you.
jokes.setup: Gang apologizing to dying Mac
jokes.punchline: Mac uses his dying words to be petty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - petty even while dying

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I... don't... forgive you.

jokes.text: Those were some pretty sweet moves. Thank you, my Lord and Savior.
jokes.setup: Mac meeting God in heaven
jokes.punchline: God casually complimenting Mac's fighting moves
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with God as a casual bro validating Mac's ego

dialogue.speaker: God
dialogue.line: Those were some pretty sweet moves.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Thank you, my Lord and Savior.

jokes.text: Oh, hey, Rex. 'Sup, bro? So what do we do now? Now we ignore everyone's prayers.
jokes.setup: Mac hanging out with God and another guy in heaven
jokes.punchline: God's job is just ignoring prayers
jokes.explanation: Dark, cynical take on divine intervention that fits the show's tone

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, hey, Rex. 'Sup, bro? So what do we do now?

dialogue.speaker: God
dialogue.line: Now we ignore everyone's prayers.

jokes.text: Damn it, Dee, this is all your fault. What? It's because of you. How is this my fault? It just is, you stupid bitch.
jokes.setup: Gang in middle of robbery
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately blames Dee for no reason with circular logic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the gang's misogynistic scapegoating of Dee

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Damn it, Dee, this is all your fault.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What? It's because of you.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: How is this my fault?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It just is, you stupid bitch.

jokes.text: Let's sacrifice her. What? Sir, sir! Please, take her, take her! Have your way with the whore!
jokes.setup: Gang deciding to sacrifice Dee
jokes.punchline: They immediately offer her to the gunman
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that shows how quickly the gang turns on each other

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Let's sacrifice her.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Sir, sir! Please, take her, take her! Have your way with the whore!

jokes.text: I'll see you in hell, boners. No witnesses. I like your style.
jokes.setup: Dee kills the robber and saves the day
jokes.punchline: Immediately plans to kill her friends as witnesses
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Dee is just as awful as the others

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'll see you in hell, boners.

dialogue.speaker: Robber
dialogue.line: No witnesses.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I like your style.

jokes.text: We should team up. Us ladies need to stick together.
jokes.setup: Dee talking to another female criminal
jokes.punchline: Bonds over gender while planning murder
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy about misapplied feminist solidarity

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: We should team up. Us ladies need to stick together.

jokes.text: Hello, love, I'm the new butler. Albert Covington's the name.
jokes.setup: Dee in witness protection
jokes.punchline: Terrible British accent as a male butler character
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of Dee's unconvincing male disguise with bad accent

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Hello, love, I'm the new butler. Albert Covington's the name.

jokes.text: Ah, ah, ah! Who slayed Achilles? Uh, Paris, Hector's brother. Well done, lad.
jokes.setup: Dee as butler teaching children
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic quiz about violent mythology
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Dee's pretentious tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Ah, ah, ah! Who slayed Achilles? Uh, Paris, Hector's brother. Well done, lad.

jokes.text: Your sweater's on backwards.
jokes.setup: Dee's elaborate butler performance
jokes.punchline: Child points out obvious costume failure
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dee's pretensions with simple observation

dialogue.line: Your sweater's on backwards.

jokes.text: Well, this time I play a very beautiful woman. Uh, a warrior princess who saves the world.
jokes.setup: Dee describing her movie role
jokes.punchline: Emphasizes playing a 'very beautiful woman' when Dee is not conventionally attractive
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy playing on Dee's delusions about her attractiveness

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, this time I play a very beautiful woman. Uh, a warrior princess who saves the world.

jokes.text: ♪ She's got blonde hair She's perfect in every way Feet not too large Not too small ♪
jokes.setup: Josh Groban singing romantic song about Dee
jokes.punchline: Awkward, unromantic lyrics about foot size
jokes.explanation: Good absurd romantic song with unromantic details

dialogue.speaker: Josh Groban
dialogue.line: ♪ She's got blonde hair She's perfect in every way Feet not too large Not too small ♪

jokes.text: That's how babies are made.
jokes.setup: Dee and Josh Groban are being romantic
jokes.punchline: Dee makes inappropriate clinical comment about reproduction
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor. Dee killing romantic moment with clinical observation is in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That's how babies are made.

jokes.text: But they didn't stay together a long time, their marriage only lasting a shocking 17 minutes
jokes.setup: Fake documentary about Dee's marriage
jokes.punchline: Absurdly short marriage duration stated matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation delivered with documentary-style deadpan

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: But they didn't stay together a long time, their marriage only lasting a shocking 17 minutes

jokes.text: A fisherman always spots another fisherman from afar.
jokes.setup: Frank talking to a robber
jokes.punchline: Inexplicable fishing metaphor for criminal recognition
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank non-sequitur wisdom that makes no sense but sounds profound

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: A fisherman always spots another fisherman from afar.

jokes.text: I dares it... I dar-dar-dar... A crammy hand... A crammy handle bit.
jokes.setup: Frank having a stroke or heart attack
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical speech that sounds like gibberish
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I dares it... I dar-dar-dar... A crammy hand... A crammy handle bit.

jokes.text: I... am... God.
jokes.setup: Frank having medical episode
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration of divinity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from medical emergency to divine delusion

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I... am... God.

jokes.text: Does-does my dick work? No. Your penis does not work. Kill me.
jokes.setup: Dennis waking up from head injury
jokes.punchline: First question is about penis, immediate death wish when it doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - sexual function is his only concern

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Does-does my dick work?

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: No. Your penis does not work.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Kill me.

jokes.text: Ah, it appears they've left.
jokes.setup: Doctor mentions Frank's friends will support his recovery
jokes.punchline: Casually notes they've already abandoned him
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor. The casual delivery of bad news - that his friends abandoned him - is nicely understated.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Ah, it appears they've left.

jokes.text: Hello, Dennis.
jokes.setup: Introduction of Dennis's rehabilitation nurse
jokes.punchline: It's a weather girl he probably exploited, now in position of power over him
jokes.explanation: Good setup for comeuppance story - Dennis's past catching up to him

dialogue.speaker: Jackie
dialogue.line: Hello, Dennis.

jokes.text: The forecast calls for sunshine.
jokes.setup: Weather girl turned nurse helping Dennis walk
jokes.punchline: Uses weather metaphor for optimistic outlook
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that connects her weather background to current situation

dialogue.line: The forecast calls for sunshine.

jokes.text: I am in love with you!
jokes.setup: Dennis's recovery montage with Jackie
jokes.punchline: Dennis declares love just as he regains ability to walk (and leave)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis only values Jackie when he needs her

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I am in love with you!

jokes.text: Her breasts? What about her breasts? I'm afraid that they were, um... obliterated.
jokes.setup: Doctor listing Jackie's injuries from accident
jokes.punchline: Dennis only cares about her breasts, which were destroyed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis characterization - shallow and horrible priorities

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Her breasts? What about her breasts?

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: I'm afraid that they were, um... obliterated.

jokes.text: Oh, get him off me! Goddamn it, stop! Whoa, easy, puppy.
jokes.setup: Police K-9 attacking the gunman
jokes.punchline: Charlie treating attack dog like a friendly pet
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's childlike nature even during violence

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, get him off me! Goddamn it, stop!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Whoa, easy, puppy.

jokes.text: All right, it looks like the hostages are dead. Let him have it.
jokes.setup: Police response to hostage situation
jokes.punchline: Immediately assume hostages are dead and open fire
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about police incompetence and casual attitude toward civilian casualties

dialogue.line: All right, it looks like the hostages are dead. Let him have it.

jokes.text: Charlie, that was so brave. Anything for you, my love.
jokes.setup: Charlie saves Waitress from gunman
jokes.punchline: Finally gets romantic validation from his obsession
jokes.explanation: Fulfills Charlie's greatest fantasy in his daydream sequence

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Charlie, that was so brave.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Anything for you, my love.

jokes.text: What are we gonna do, you guys? Are you thinking what I'm thinking? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Gang in crisis asking for solutions
jokes.punchline: Cut to them trying to rob the register themselves
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline showing the gang's moral bankruptcy - they decide to rob the place themselves

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What are we gonna do, you guys?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you thinking what I'm thinking?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: The big bills, underneath. Give me the big bills.
jokes.setup: Gang wonders what they should do about robbery
jokes.punchline: Cut to reality - they're just complying with robber's demands
jokes.explanation: Excellent reality check. After all the fantasy sequences, the actual resolution is they just give the robber money like normal people. Good structural payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Robber
dialogue.line: The big bills, underneath. Give me the big bills.

tmdb_overview: A routine trip to a convenience store places the gang in the middle of an armed robbery.
